titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/242

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=212 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:28:44.299643+00 | translation moa=213 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:28:45.860028+00

div.p:
.W. dei gratia Rex Scott’. Omnibus probis hominibus totius terre sue Clericis ⁊ laicis  salutem. Sciant
presentes . ⁊ futuri me concessisse  ⁊ hac carta mea confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie sancte crucis de
Edenburg . ⁊ canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus illam carrucatam terre . ⁊ decem acras
in territorio de Trauernent . uersus Phauheside . quam Rob’. de Quinci eis dedit pro excambio
illius Carrucata terre de prestune quam idem canonici uersus eundem Rob’. ⁊ monachos
de Neubothle calumniauerunt . Tenend’ . sibi . in liberam . ⁊ puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam
cum libero exitu . ⁊ comuni pastura de Trauernent . quantum ad tantum terre pertinet . ita libere . ⁊ Quiete . plenarie . ⁊ honorifice  sicut Carta predicti Rob’. testatur  saluo ser
uicio meo . Test’. Ric’. de prebend’. clerico meo . Rob’. de Quinci . Philipp’. de Mubrahi .
Willelmo. de lindes’ . Willelmo de Vallibus . Herb’. de Camera . philippo. de londin’ . Waltero
Murdach. Apud Hadingtun .xix. die Augusti.

William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting: Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood at Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there, the ploughgate of land and ten acres in the territory of Tranent, towards Falside, that Robert de Quincy has given to them for the exchange of the ploughgate of land at Preston that the same canons claimed against the same Robert and the monks of Newbattle. For them to hold in free and pure and perpetual alms with free exit and the common pasture of Tranent, as much as belongs to such a land, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably as the charter of the said Robert bears witness, reserving my service. As witness, Richard de Prebenda my clerk, Robert de Quincy, Philip de Mowbray, William of Lindsay, William de Vaux, Herbert de Camera, Philip of Lundin, Walter Murdoch. At Haddington on the 19th day of August.
